codebaseHealth.structure: Good - The project follows Next.js 14 App Router conventions with clear separation of concerns. Components are in /components, utilities in /lib, and routes follow the app directory structure. However, there's minimal component reusability and no dedicated utilities or hooks directories.
codebaseHealth.codeQuality: Fair - Code is generally clean and follows TypeScript best practices. However, there are several issues: (1) No error boundaries implemented, (2) Inconsistent error handling patterns, (3) Missing loading states in many components, (4) No code splitting or lazy loading, (5) Hardcoded values scattered throughout (e.g., workout types in upload/page.tsx), (6) No centralized constants file, (7) Type definitions are scattered and incomplete (missing next-auth.d.ts content verification needed).
technicalDebt: No automated tests (0% test coverage) - no Jest, Vitest, or testing library configured
Missing error boundaries - unhandled errors will crash the entire app
No loading/skeleton states for async operations - poor UX during data fetching
Hardcoded configuration values (workout types, pricing, limits) - should be in config files
No logging/monitoring infrastructure (no Sentry, LogRocket, etc.)
Missing database migrations tracking - no migration history or versioning
No API rate limiting or request throttling
No input sanitization for XSS prevention on user-generated content
Prisma client initialization without connection pooling optimization
No bundle size analysis or optimization
Missing TypeScript strict mode optimizations (some 'any' types likely present)
No environment variable validation on startup
Email transporter created on every import instead of singleton pattern
S3 client recreated on each use instead of reusing connections
codebaseHealth.maintainability: Fair - Well-organized file structure makes it easy to find code, but lack of documentation in code, missing JSDoc comments, and no component prop documentation hurt maintainability. No contribution guidelines or development setup automation.

architectureReview.currentArchitecture: Next.js 14 App Router full-stack application with: Frontend - React 18 with Server Components and Client Components, TailwindCSS for styling. Backend - Next.js API Routes for REST endpoints, Prisma ORM with PostgreSQL. Authentication - NextAuth.js with JWT sessions and credentials provider. File Storage - AWS S3 with presigned URLs. Payments - Stripe Checkout with webhook handling. Email - NodeMailer with SMTP. State Management - TanStack Query for server state, React hooks for local state.
issues: No API versioning strategy - breaking changes will affect all clients
Missing pagination on list endpoints (videos, feedback) - will cause performance issues at scale
No caching layer (Redis) for frequently accessed data like trainer lists
Database queries not optimized - N+1 query potential in feedback/video relationships
No CDN configured for static assets
Missing database indexes on commonly filtered fields (createdAt on several tables)
No separation of concerns between business logic and route handlers - all logic in route files
No service layer or repository pattern - database calls scattered throughout
File upload uses presigned URLs but no cleanup job for abandoned uploads
No retry logic for failed email sends or webhook processing
Session strategy uses JWT but no refresh token mechanism - users must re-login when tokens expire
No WebSocket implementation for real-time updates (trainer notifications, feedback status)
opportunities: Implement tRPC for type-safe API calls instead of fetch with manual typing
Add React Query devtools for better debugging
Implement incremental static regeneration (ISR) for trainer listings page
Add edge functions for authentication middleware (faster global performance)
Implement image optimization with next/image for trainer avatars and thumbnails
Add server actions for form submissions (simpler than API routes)
Implement middleware-based request logging and monitoring
Use Prisma Client Extensions for reusable query logic
Implement optimistic updates with React Query for better UX
Add Suspense boundaries for better loading states
Implement parallel route segments for complex dashboards
Use Next.js metadata API more extensively for SEO

designReview.designSystem: Fair - Custom Tailwind utility classes defined (btn, card, input) provide consistency. Color palette is defined with primary and accent colors. However: (1) No design tokens file, (2) Inconsistent spacing usage, (3) No typography scale defined, (4) No component variants documented, (5) No Storybook or component library documentation.
uiIssues: No favicon or app icons defined
Landing page lacks visual interest - no images, videos, or testimonials
No empty states with actionable CTAs for most pages
Forms lack real-time validation feedback - errors only shown on submit
No confirmation dialogs for destructive actions
Missing breadcrumb navigation on nested pages
No visual feedback for successful actions (toasts/notifications)
Trainer cards lack profile images - text-only is less engaging
Video player not implemented - just shows 'fileUrl' without actual playback
Dashboard lacks visual hierarchy - everything looks same importance
No dark mode support despite CSS variables being defined
Mobile responsiveness not tested - likely has issues on small screens
No loading spinners or progress indicators during async operations
Form inputs lack proper focus states and accessibility styling
uxIssues: No onboarding flow for new users - dropped into empty dashboard
Credit purchase flow is unclear - no pricing page or packages displayed
No way to preview trainer profiles before selecting
Feedback submission has no autosave - users lose work if they navigate away
No search or filter functionality on trainer listings
No ability to edit or delete uploaded videos
Missing user profile settings page - can't update name, email, password
No notification system - users don't know when feedback is ready without checking
Trainer selection is one-way - users can't change trainers after selection
No way to view feedback history or previous reviews
Missing pagination on dashboard - only shows 10 videos
No video upload progress indicator - users don't know if large files are uploading
Credit balance not prominently displayed - users might not know they need to purchase
No way to request refunds or dispute feedback
Missing help/support documentation or contact information
accessibilityIssues: No ARIA labels on interactive elements
Form inputs missing associated labels (some use htmlFor, others don't)
Color contrast not verified - may fail WCAG AA standards
Focus indicators missing or unclear on interactive elements
No skip-to-content link for keyboard users
Error messages not announced to screen readers
Modal/dialog elements missing proper ARIA roles and focus trapping
No alt text strategy for future images
Video player would lack captions/subtitles support
No reduced motion support for animations

featureGaps: No video playback functionality - currently just stores URLs without player
Missing timestamped comments feature mentioned in landing page but not implemented
No trainer rating system for users to rate trainers after feedback
No subscription tier system mentioned in README Phase 2
Missing admin panel for platform management
No analytics dashboard for trainers to track performance
No user profile editing or account management
Missing email verification flow - users can register with invalid emails
No notification preferences or email unsubscribe
No referral or credit reward system
No dispute resolution or refund system
No messaging system between users and trainers
No mobile app despite being mentioned in future enhancements
No AI-powered form analysis mentioned in roadmap
performanceIssues: No image optimization - next/image not used anywhere
Bundle size not analyzed - likely shipping too much JavaScript
No code splitting beyond default Next.js behavior
TanStack Query configured with short staleTime (60s) - could be optimized per-query
Database queries load unnecessary relations - over-fetching data
No server-side caching for expensive queries (trainer listings)
Prisma queries not using select to limit fields - fetching entire objects
No lazy loading of components or routes
Email transporter instantiated on module load instead of lazy initialization
No compression middleware for API responses
Large video files could overwhelm server without proper streaming
No database connection pooling configuration visible
Middleware runs on every request without optimization
No prefetching of likely next pages (e.g., trainers page from dashboard)
CSS utility classes could benefit from PurgeCSS verification
securityIssues: NextAuth secret defaults to weak value in .env.example
No rate limiting on authentication endpoints - vulnerable to brute force
No CSRF protection beyond NextAuth defaults
API routes don't validate content-type headers
No SQL injection protection verification (Prisma should handle, but not tested)
Environment variables not validated on startup - app could run with missing critical vars
Stripe webhook doesn't verify webhook signature properly in production (uses || '' default)
AWS credentials fallback to empty strings instead of throwing errors
No input sanitization for user-generated content (XSS risk in feedback/comments)
File upload accepts any file type despite claiming video-only (client-side only)
No file size validation on server-side for uploads
Presigned URLs don't expire appropriately (1 hour is long for upload)
No Content Security Policy headers configured
Password validation only checks length - no complexity requirements
No account lockout after failed login attempts
Session tokens don't rotate on privilege escalation
Trainer role can be set by anyone during registration (no verification process)
No audit logging for sensitive operations

testCoverage.current: 0% - No test files found. No testing framework configured.
gaps: No unit tests for utility functions (validations, auth, s3, stripe, email)
No integration tests for API routes
No E2E tests for critical user flows (signup, upload, feedback)
No component tests for React components
No database seed data for testing
No CI/CD pipeline with automated testing
No performance testing or load testing
No mocking strategy for external services (S3, Stripe, email)

prioritizedImprovements.title: Implement comprehensive input validation and sanitization
prioritizedImprovements.description: Add server-side validation for all user inputs, sanitize HTML content in feedback and comments to prevent XSS attacks, validate file types and sizes on server-side, and add rate limiting to all public endpoints especially auth routes.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Add environment variable validation on startup
prioritizedImprovements.description: Create a startup validation script that checks all required environment variables are present and valid before the application starts. Use a library like zod or joi to validate env vars. Fail fast with clear error messages if configuration is incomplete.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Implement actual video playback functionality
prioritizedImprovements.description: Currently videos are uploaded but there's no player to view them. Integrate a video player library (video.js, Plyr, or react-player) that supports S3 presigned URLs. Add playback controls, quality selection, and playback speed controls. This is critical for the core functionality.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Add error boundaries to prevent app crashes
prioritizedImprovements.description: Implement React error boundaries at the root layout and major page sections to catch runtime errors gracefully. Display user-friendly error messages instead of crashing. Log errors to monitoring service for debugging.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Fix Stripe webhook signature verification
prioritizedImprovements.description: The webhook route uses empty string fallback for STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET which will fail in production. Make this a required env var, validate it exists on startup, and remove the fallback. Add proper error logging for webhook failures.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Implement credit purchase pricing page and flow
prioritizedImprovements.description: Create a dedicated /credits or /pricing page that shows available credit packages with clear pricing. Implement the full purchase flow with package selection, Stripe checkout, and success/failure handling. Currently the credits page is referenced but doesn't exist.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Add comprehensive loading and error states
prioritizedImprovements.description: Implement loading skeletons for all async data fetching, show progress indicators for uploads, add error states with retry buttons for failed operations. Use React Suspense where appropriate. This dramatically improves perceived performance.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Implement timestamped video comments
prioritizedImprovements.description: The landing page promises 'timestamped comments' but this isn't implemented. Add ability for trainers to add comments linked to specific video timestamps. Users should be able to click comments and jump to that point in the video. This is a key differentiator.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Add pagination and infinite scroll to list pages
prioritizedImprovements.description: Implement cursor-based pagination for videos, feedback, and trainers lists. Currently queries use 'take' limits without pagination which will break at scale. Add infinite scroll on frontend with React Query for smooth UX.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Implement email verification and password reset
prioritizedImprovements.description: Add email verification flow with verification tokens sent after signup. Users must verify email before uploading videos. Add forgot password flow with secure reset tokens. These are essential security features for production.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Build user profile and settings page
prioritizedImprovements.description: Create /settings route where users can update name, email, password, notification preferences, and fitness goals. Trainers should be able to edit bio, specialties, certifications, and hourly rate. Currently no way to edit profile after signup.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Fix Trainer relation queries in feedback routes
prioritizedImprovements.description: In /app/api/feedback/route.ts lines 28-31, the trainer relation select tries to access 'name' and 'role' but Trainer model doesn't have these - they're on the User model. This will cause runtime errors. Fix the nested relation query.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Add real-time notification system
prioritizedImprovements.description: Implement toast notifications for user actions (upload success, feedback received, credit purchase). Consider WebSocket or polling for real-time updates when trainers submit feedback. Add notification bell icon in navbar with unread count.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Implement trainer rating and review system
prioritizedImprovements.description: After receiving feedback, allow users to rate trainers (1-5 stars) and write reviews. Display ratings on trainer cards and profile pages. Update trainer.rating calculation based on user reviews instead of defaulting to 0.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Add video management features (edit, delete, privacy)
prioritizedImprovements.description: Allow users to edit video titles/descriptions, delete videos they've uploaded, and set privacy settings. Add confirmation modals for destructive actions. Implement proper cascade deletes with cleanup of S3 files.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Optimize database queries and add caching
prioritizedImprovements.description: Add Redis caching layer for trainer listings, user credits, and other frequently accessed data. Optimize Prisma queries to use select() for specific fields instead of fetching entire objects. Add database indexes on filtered columns.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Create comprehensive onboarding flow
prioritizedImprovements.description: Design multi-step onboarding for new users: welcome message, profile completion, first video upload tutorial, trainer selection guide. For trainers: certification upload, profile setup, payout configuration. Use a library like react-joyride for guided tours.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Build admin dashboard for platform management
prioritizedImprovements.description: Create admin-only routes for: user management, trainer verification, platform analytics, content moderation, refund processing. Add admin role to User enum and protect routes. Essential for scaling the platform.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Add search and advanced filtering for trainers
prioritizedImprovements.description: Implement search by name, filter by specialties, certifications, rating, price range, and availability. Add sorting options (rating, reviews, price). Use Prisma full-text search or integrate Algolia/Elasticsearch for better search.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Implement proper file cleanup for abandoned uploads
prioritizedImprovements.description: Create a cleanup job that removes S3 files for videos that were uploaded via presigned URL but never had a Video record created. Run as a scheduled task (cron job or serverless function) to prevent storage bloat.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Add comprehensive testing infrastructure
prioritizedImprovements.description: Set up Jest/Vitest with React Testing Library for component tests. Add Playwright or Cypress for E2E tests covering critical flows. Implement API route testing. Set up test database with seed data. Add CI/CD pipeline with automated testing on PRs.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Improve mobile responsiveness and touch interactions
prioritizedImprovements.description: Audit all pages on mobile devices, fix layout issues, ensure touch targets are minimum 44x44px, improve form inputs for mobile, test on iOS and Android. Add mobile-specific optimizations like bottom navigation.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Implement video processing pipeline
prioritizedImprovements.description: Add server-side video processing: generate thumbnails, compress videos, convert to web-optimized formats (H264/VP9), create multiple quality versions. Use FFmpeg or cloud service like Mux/Cloudinary. Improves load times and bandwidth.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Add comprehensive security headers and CSP
prioritizedImprovements.description: Implement Content Security Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, and other security headers. Use next.config.js headers configuration or middleware. Protect against common web vulnerabilities.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Enhance landing page with social proof and visuals
prioritizedImprovements.description: Add hero image/video, trainer testimonials, user success stories, trust badges (certifications), demo video, before/after comparisons. Add FAQ section. Improve copy with stronger CTAs. This directly impacts conversion rates.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Optimize bundle size and implement code splitting
prioritizedImprovements.description: Analyze bundle with @next/bundle-analyzer, lazy load heavy components (video player, react-dropzone), implement route-based code splitting, remove unused dependencies. Set up performance budgets in CI.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Add trainer availability and scheduling system
prioritizedImprovements.description: Allow trainers to set available hours and maximum reviews per day. Users see estimated turnaround time before selecting trainer. Implement queue system to distribute work fairly. Prevents trainer burnout and sets expectations.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Implement autosave for feedback composition
prioritizedImprovements.description: Auto-save trainer feedback to localStorage or database every 30 seconds as they type. Restore draft if they navigate away and return. Add visual indicator showing 'Saving...', 'Saved', or 'Unsaved changes'. Prevents data loss.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Add analytics and monitoring infrastructure
prioritizedImprovements.description: Integrate Sentry or similar for error tracking, add application performance monitoring (APM), implement custom analytics for business metrics (conversion rates, retention, revenue). Add dashboard for key metrics. Essential for data-driven decisions.

prioritizedImprovements.title: Improve accessibility to WCAG 2.1 AA compliance
prioritizedImprovements.description: Add proper ARIA labels, ensure keyboard navigation works, verify color contrast ratios, add screen reader testing, implement focus management, add alt text strategy, support reduced motion preferences. Use axe-core or Pa11y for automated testing.
